summary: Additive wiring of an existing, fully-tested quality tier module into the render pipeline. No new architectural patterns introduced; all changes are configuration-layer additions with safe defaults and backward compatibility.
recommendations: Store the detach function returned by attachResizeHandler so the old handler can be cleanly removed before re-attaching with resolutionScale.
computeQuality is a pure synchronous function — consider calling it immediately after readSignals() inside the async block rather than adding another async boundary.
Ensure the quick antialias heuristic (DPR ≤ 1 && touch) is documented with a comment explaining why it diverges from the full quality computation.

files_impact.src/main.ts: moderate — orchestration hub; uncomment imports, wire quality computation, create geometry systems, re-apply resolution scale
files_impact.src/visual/scene.ts: low — activate unused _resolutionScale parameter, add disableAntialias option
files_impact.src/visual/resize.ts: low — add optional resolutionScale parameter to preserve scaled pixel ratio on resize
files_impact.src/visual/renderLoop.ts: minimal — add optional quality field to LoopDeps interface
files_impact.src/visual/systems/particleField.ts: none — already accepts maxParticles and enableSparkle config
files_impact.src/visual/systems/waveField.ts: none — already accepts maxWaves and enableShimmer config
files_impact.src/visual/modeManager.ts: none — already implemented, ready for wiring
files_impact.src/visual/types.ts: none — GeometrySystem and FrameState interfaces unchanged

test_coverage_assessment: adequate — 8 existing unit tests cover tier computation; 3 planned integration tests cover config flow to geometry systems and renderer
dependency_safety: all dependencies (US-003, US-004, US-009, US-013) provide the modules this plan wires together; no circular or missing dependencies
